name: Team Cory
subject: Choose love over hate
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CORY BOOKER FOR SENATE
body:
How we weather this storm |
#
Friends, We hope you're hanging in there. We just wanted to reach out real quick and send everyone a video message from Cory sharing his thoughts on how we get through these uncertain and challenging times together. [**Click here to see the video. >>**](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e64/5fca0d19/7a66fd32/291d1379/4003104228/VEsH/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6CsyHTHSncU&feature=youtu.be") [](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e64/5fca0d19/7a66fd32/291d1379/4003104228/VEsE/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=6CsyHTHSncU&feature=youtu.be") We know he'd love to hear what's on your mind as well. Feel free to record your own video and either send it to the team by replying to this email or tweeting it directly to **[@CoryBooker](https://act.corybooker.com/page/m/4fdc0e64/5fca0d19/7a66fd32/291d1378/4003104228/VEsF/?g=IiwAaFH8x8VcsCn-MvZWseA "http://twitter.com/corybooker")**. Stay safe, Team Cory

---

name: Joe Kennedy III
subject: We all like to say that this disease doesn't discriminate. It does.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kennedy for Massachusetts
body:
| [](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639011e/55b66746/23a6c8fc/80e06ac/147359825/VEsH/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "http://www.kennedyforma.com/")
---

| | We all like to say that this disease doesn't discriminate. But in a deeply unjust healthcare system, COVID-19 is hyper-targeted at communities of color, low-income communities, immigrant communities, and those working on the frontlines. **Right now, residents in Chelsea are being told to stay inside 24 hours a day.** There are 96 cases in the city per 10,000 residents -- the highest proportion anywhere in the state, and a higher proportion than even New York City. The next highest rate in the state is 60 cases per 10,000 residents. Overwhelmingly, these hotspots are communities of color. Why? Because generations of systemic injustice compound all the risk factors for COVID-19. Discrimination in housing, health care, transportation, and environmental policy puts black and brown families directly in harm's way - working jobs that don't give sick days, taking public transit that doesn't offer adequate protection, breathing polluted air that weakens hearts and lungs. In Chelsea, a group of local residents, leaders and organizations have stepped up to provide support and access to food and shelter, in what is becoming an increasingly acute crisis. Will you join me in supporting the Chelsea Collaborative, which is helping run this effort on the ground? **Donate to the Chelsea Collaborative** Join me in giving to families in one
of Massachusetts's hardest-hit areas. [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639011e/55b66746/23a6c8fc/80e06ae/147359825/VEsE/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-chelseacollab?refcode=2020-04-09-chelsea&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") Chelsea has one of the largest immigrant populations in the state. This means many families are restricted from traditional public benefits and nutrition assistance - and are afraid of seeking the care they need because of fear they could be deported. We need to step up for them. Not only because it is the right thing to do. But because taking care of each other - and taking care of those especially vulnerable at this moment - is the only way we will get through this crisis. Communities like Chelsea are home to the workers carrying what is left of our economy right now - the first responders, grocery workers, delivery men and women, transit workers, janitors, and countless others. We need them. And right now they need us. If you can, please consider pitching in to this community. We'll get through this together.
**Donate to the Chelsea Collaborative** [Donate](https://secure.kennedyforma.com/page/m/3639011e/55b66746/23a6c8fc/80e06ae/147359825/VEsF/p/eyJlM3RoYzJ0OWZRPT0iOiIzIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjl2Ym1WOWZRPT0iOiI1IiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjkwZDI5OWZRPT0iOiIxNSIsImUzdGhZbUZ0ZEY5MGFISmxaWDE5IjoiMjUiLCJlM3RoWW1GdGRGOW1iM1Z5ZlgwPSI6IjUwIiwiZTN0aFltRnRkRjltYVhabGZYMD0iOiIxMDAifQ%3D%3D/?g=wJ6aEDjfd5ldgcs4t9rIVg "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kennedy-chelseacollab?refcode=2020-04-09-chelsea&recurring=0&amount=3&amounts=5,15,25,50,100") |

These are just a few of the questions we posed to Dr. Josh Sharfstein -- an expert on public health emergencies -- in the latest episode of our Follow the Data podcast. Dr. Sharfstein is the Vice Dean for Public Health Practice and Community Engagement at the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health. Jessica Leighton, from the Bloomberg Philanthropies Public Health team, sat down with him to get expert insights on the unprecedented response efforts happening across the country. Listen to their full conversation on Follow the Data, available now on these platforms: Soundcloud: Spotify: Apple Podcasts: Stitcher: Simplecast: Thanks, \- Bloomberg Philanthropies | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- . Bloomberg Philanthropies All content © 2020. All rights reserved.

name: Jason Yates
subject: Good Friday: How to prepare communion at home
body:
| | Peter, Christians across the country are remembering Good Friday differently this year. Instead of gathering together at church, most are reflecting on Christ’s sacrifice from home. Even though we may not be joining together corporately with other believers, we should not skip out on setting aside time to remember what Christ has done for us. **World Outreach Church has put together a great and simple video offering suggestions for preparing the communion elements at home and making it a meaningful worship experience.** [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=fhHaA66SN4XE9EANOi3uQchci7TRSZgtZNJ6f33Tl9ttfAIzsaLZXAkukfZv17XWv1kUa2vLhYNpU5A9oETzAA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200410com01&n=1) View this helpful video here: [https://wochurch.org/easter-service](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=fhHaA66SN4XE9EANOi3uQchci7TRSZgtZNJ6f33Tl9ttfAIzsaLZXAkukfZv17XWv1kUa2vLhYNpU5A9oETzAA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200410com01&n=2) You and your family can put this into practice and join us and our good friend, Pastor Allen Jackson of World Outreach Church, as he leads us in a special Good Friday Communion Service tonight. **Watch the Communion Service Live at 7:00PM ET and 4:00PM PT here:** [https://wochurch.org/live](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=3cknPF5iDZktj398w-4IYoGT4pGTqLSxn9mz0hpCERY&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200410com01&n=3) We are grateful to be connected to you and worship our Savior together for all he has done (Romans 5:6-9). **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e200410com01&n=4)
---

name: Nina Ahmad
subject: This Pandemic Will Change Us - Leadership Will Matter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina for PA
body:
| | With over 18,000 cases of coronavirus and over 300 tragic deaths in Pennsylvania as of today, all of us are concerned for the health and economic well-being of our families, friends and community. Maybe more than anything, we wonder about the future. This pandemic will change us. In these circumstances, it is understandable that a lot of us are not thinking about politics. But I suggest that this is a moment more than ever when the political decisions we make will profoundly affect the future. Democrats know politics at its best is about _hope_. We can all see plainly that smart, thoughtful and committed leadership matters. What we learn, say and share can make a real difference now and going forward. That's why as Pennsylvania's Auditor General, I will make sure that all of us have a voice in the rooms where the future will be decided. Not just special interests and insiders, but _all_ of us. With that in mind, my health plan specifically calls for a comprehensive health audit, to assess readiness incorporating the lessons learned, not just my own as a woman and medical scientist, but from a broad base of those on the front lines of the COVID-19 fight: healthcare workers, public health and supply chain experts, economists, labor and community organizers, and patients. Many of them are already our supporters: this week our campaign was endorsed by SEIU Local 1199C, a union of tens of thousands of healthcare heroes. **It is critical that we raise our voices now. We can make a difference. Please join us. I 'm asking for your help today.** [**If you are able, your donation is greatly appreciated. Click HERE.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/OvNomrJY_BnFX8zbP-DhRqV_LqrTRG-nvAfk-hf1zH0=) Thanks for your support, Nina [**PS - please contribute what you can, safely and securely, by clicking HERE.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/OvNomrJY_BnFX8zbP-DhRqV_LqrTRG-nvAfk-hf1zH0=) Learn more by visiting our website: [**_www.NinaForPA.com_**](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/DVCUmH4oCfuoHfECqA9tSe8IDVO1OR7ZnA_DJZUO8IE=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/OvNomrJY_BnFX8zbP-DhRqV_LqrTRG-nvAfk-hf1zH0=) Follow us on Social Media: ** ** [](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/1128nN1FoDg5EX2yMx3iugXj35GpJ7Lp--IdVi-EYPI=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/So4iX2VczTMK0tV-ihQ1Nvq7bpGngz0iEfmBNtcMjk0=) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/xURYDin7S7aC1yjvp6uLX2moer01KdGeN9mOTbww9M8=/k8_03rWojB6f9jtw-sEg4KxYre7wjdtV-v-O2UsYsSA=?hl=en)

name: Team Hicks
subject: After yesterday’s planning meeting, we’re ramping up our volunteering options
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
body:
Friends —

Over the last few weeks, we’ve talked a lot about how our campaign is adapting — our team is practicing social distancing and we’ve canceled all in-person events.

Normally, we’re out driving from corner to corner of our district so Josh can hear from as many people as possible.

With a “Healthy at Home” order, our team is getting creative, and we’re ready to let you know how you can get involved in growing #TeamHicks!
We’re looking for folks who are interested in helping out — whether that’s sewing face masks for essential workers, volunteering with local non-profits, or helping spread the word about Josh and our campaign through social media.

No matter what role you’re able to take on, our volunteer teams will help us get more folks engaged and harness the power of our grassroots supporters (that’s you!).

Next , we need you to sign up to join one or several of our volunteer teams.

Whether you’re an expert seamstress or a savvy social media user, we need your help. And if you have other ideas, we want to hear those, too.

SIGN UP TO VOLUNTEER! [https://secure.ngpvan.com/r5iYnMTLcEWy_tZ9nNQI2A2]

Thank you for being a part of this team — no matter how you can get involved. Whether that’s reading this email, becoming a donor, or joining one of our #TeamHicks volunteer teams, we’re proud to have you on board.

-- Team Hicks

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-apr?refcode=HIC_20200410_volsurvey_em_org_all_ftr1 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-apr?refcode=HIC_20200410_em_volsurvey_org_all_ftr1]

Paid For By Josh Hicks for Congress
Josh Hicks for Congress
PO Box 1214
Lexington, KY 40588
United States

Josh Hicks is a Democrat running to represent Kentucky’s 6th Congressional District. He will be a representative who puts the needs of his constituents front and center in Washington. Josh grew up in Fleming County before leaving to serve in the Marines. After two deployments, he returned to Kentucky to serve his community as a police officer and attorney.
He’s running to represent hardworking, everyday Kentuckians. Not corporations. That means his campaign is relying on grassroots donations — lots of small donations from emails like this — not big checks from corporate special interests. Please consider making a $5 contribution to Josh’s campaign today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-apr?amount=5&refcode=HIC_20200410_volsurvey_em_org_all_ftr2]

CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jhicks-apr?refcode=HIC_20200410_volsurvey_em_org_all_ftr3]

name: MJ Hegar HQ
subject: the least-insured state in the country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MJ for Texas
body:
As we all face this pandemic together, it's clear that Texans urgently need a Senator who will fight to make sure that everyone can get the health care they need.

|
---
[](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489039b/7f51f7bc/4ca2d060/4ef13d3f/760723736/VEsH/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/logo?refcode=emaillogo&amount=5")

| | **_"TEXAS HAS THE MOST PEOPLE
WITHOUT HEALTH INSURANCE IN THE NATION"_**
\-- _The Texas Tribune_ **[Pitch in to elect a senator who will fight to get EVERY Texan the care they need!](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489039b/7f51f7bc/4ca2d060/4ef13d3d/760723736/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExOTMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-uninsured?refcode=email_20200410.1nd__1193__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

Peter -- Texas is already the least-insured state in the country, and **Sen. John Cornyn is standing by in silence while his political allies push forward a lawsuit that would rip coverage from nearly 2 million _more_ Texans with pre-existing conditions!**

As we all face this pandemic together, it's clear that **Texans urgently need a senator who will fight to make sure that everyone can get the health care they need** \-- not someone who's in the pocket of Big Pharma and the insurance industry.

That's why it's crucial that we do everything we can to help MJ defeat Sen. John Cornyn this November -- **[but we can't do it without your help!](https://secure.mjfortexas.com/page/m/6489039b/7f51f7bc/4ca2d060/4ef13d3d/760723736/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZOUVVsTVNVNUhTVVFsSlE9PSI6IjExOTMifQ%3D%3D/?g=f7VweDpTVzRduSR3BPpGv5A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mj-uninsured?refcode=email_20200410.1nd__1193__&amount=10&recurring=true")**

While Sen. Cornyn has taken millions from pharmaceutical companies and the insurance industry, **MJ isn't taking _any_ corporate PAC money.** She's counting on your grassroots support -- and that's how you _know_ she'll fight for you in the Senate.

subject: Help me stop the do-nothing Dems

name: Hickenlooper.com
subject: FWD: Sign my petition to demand the 2020 election be vote by mail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
body:
|
#
| | Folks, as our country struggles with an unprecedented public health crisis, we need to make sure we uphold democracy — and protect people’s safety at the same time. While Donald Trump has been attacking vote by mail _(even though he voted by mail himself in 2018 AND just last month)_ , Colorado has been successfully voting by mail for years now. [**No one should have to choose between their health and their Constitutional right to vote. Read John’s message below and then sign our petition to demand vote by mail nationwide in the 2020 election.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103348/182451294/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715) | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103350/182451296/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103353/182451299/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
Hello —
The coronavirus outbreak is forcing all Americans to adapt to new at-home routines, from YouTube workouts to conference calls, home schooling, and video chats with our friends across the street and across the country.
**Our democracy is no different. Every eligible voter should be able to make their voice heard in our elections without facing the long lines, crowds, and health risks of in-person polling places. **
Now is the time to recognize the impact this pandemic is having on our elections and democracy, and come together to address it before it’s too late:
[**If you agree, sign my urgent petition demanding President Trump and Congress work together to ensure that every American can vote by mail in the 2020 election and beyond.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103355/182451301/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103357/182451303/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
---
If you saw what happened in Wisconsin’s elections on Tuesday, you saw a system that was fundamentally flawed.
Local election officials and volunteers, typically seniors, were desperately hunting down sanitizer while hundreds of polling places were closed due to health concerns — but Republicans still fought tooth and nail to stop Wisconsin’s Governor Evers from postponing the election to protect the public’s health.
_It was sheer chaos! _
There’s got to be a better way to protect public health and our democracy. And in fact, we’ve already found one here in Colorado where we’ve been voting from home by mail for the last few years.
[**It’s time to bring that system nationwide and use Colorado as a model for our country once again. Sign my petition if you agree we need vote by mail in every state for the 2020 elections.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103359/182451305/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17103361/182451307/-328326912?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&emci=891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=7280715)
---
Universal vote at home reform is simply common sense. Unfortunately, common sense does not prevail very often in Washington. To that I say: it’s time we make Washington a little more like Colorado. We’ve proven that vote at home reforms work, so why not expand them to the rest of the United States?
Our democracy and our public health are too important for anything less.
Thanks for taking action to protect our elections.
— John Hickenlooper
Paid for by Hickenlooper for Colorado
---
Hickenlooper for Colorado
PO Box 18886
Denver, CO 80218
United States
[unsubscribe](https://email.ngpvan.com/unsubscribeUnique/891f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8/8a1f1f3a-557b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83MjY1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTFmMWYzYS01NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=T3UM_XQzI-onsOO_7Z51lPE7Q7ghy5MHbQ1fYl6MWEc=&id=109201661&emailAddress=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: Sacrifice and hope
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
During this Holy Week, Hoosiers across the state -- including my family -- are reflecting. We're spending time reflecting on the sacrifice Jesus made | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/sacrifice-and-hope?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_Sacrifice & Hope_** During this Holy Week, Hoosiers across the state -- including my family -- are reflecting. We're spending time reflecting on the sacrifice Jesus made for us all, but also the service and sacrifice our fellow Hoosiers are making during this challenging time.

It’s our doctors, nurses and other medical professionals putting themselves in harm’s way to help those in need. It’s our farmers, truck drivers and grocery clerks making sure we still have access to the necessities of life. It's our police officers and firefighters continuing to report for duty, keeping our communities safe. It’s other essential employees doing their job to make sure our state keeps operating.

And, really, it's every Hoosier doing what needs to be done -- hunkering down at home and social distancing to save lives. After all, as **Governor Holcomb** has been saying, we're _in this together_.

As we look to Easter this Sunday, I’m filled with hope. Just as Jesus was resurrected and lives, I know that soon our state and our communities will come back stronger than ever. Happy Easter.

\- Chairman Kyle Hupfer
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4606b25174&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Happy Passover, Hoosiers!**_
Happy Passover to all Hoosiers of the Jewish faith! We hope that you are able to celebrate this important time at home with your family.
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
|
---
| _**Keeping Hoosiers Safe: Coronavirus**_ **Governor Holcomb** continues to take action to lead our state during this challenging time.

This week, he extended Indiana’s Hunker Down Hoosiers Stay-at-Home Order. He also identified businesses that provide the necessities of life, and how businesses should operate moving forward during this order. Additionally, he provided updates on Indiana's infrastructure plans. With less traffic on the roads, INDOT is getting a jumpstart on its construction plans.

Recap of this week's news:
* **Extension of Indiana’s Hunker Down Hoosiers Stay-at-Home Order:**
* [Frequently asked questions answered here.](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=556f507e10&e=1acd9bb3d6)
* Retail business that provide necessities of life may stay open if they:
* Limit the number of customers in a store at any one time.
* Adjust hours to accommodate vulnerable populations, such as the elderly.
* Limit hours so they can restock and clean.
* Comply with all mitigation measures to protect employees and customers.
* Retail business not providing necessities of life may stay open if they:
* Restrict sales to online or call-in ordering with delivery or curbside pickup.
* Businesses must comply with social distancing, sanitation and other mitigation measures to protect employees.
* **Infrastructure Updates:**
* Vehicle traffic is down 40% during the week and over 50% on the weekends!
* Community Crossings grants were awarded this week, which assist local communities with the repair, ongoing maintenance, and improvement of their roads and bridges.
* 217 Community Crossing grants are being awarded in 61 counties and 156 cities and towns...for a total of $126.5 million in matching grants! [Check here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=28af9ddfce&e=1acd9bb3d6) to see if your county or community has been awarded funds!
* And with fewer Hoosiers on the roads, INDOT is jumpstarting road construction across the state. This includes:
* I-70 on the east side of Indianapolis will be closed for major repairs. Originally scheduled to take five months to complete, it will now be done in one month.
* Section Six of I-69 (between Martinsville and Indianapolis) continues to move forward as scheduled.
* Projects are occurring all over the state, including the Lloyd Expressway in Evansville and I-469 in Fort Wayne.

To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make you're following**Governor Holcomb** on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=deb12fc608&e=1acd9bb3d6), [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3ec9a3cab1&e=1acd9bb3d6) and [Instagram](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ac82d11c9d&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

Thank you to every Hoosier who is doing his or her part to stop the spread of the coronavirus and flatten the curve. We're #INThisTogether!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ede1be8c87&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Resources: A State That Works Together**_
As news and information on the coronavirus continue to rapidly change -- we want to make sure you have the resources you need to stay up to date!
* **Indiana State Department of Health** \- The Indiana State Department of Health is posting important information, and frequent updates to their website. [Check out their website here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0158957e7b&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
* **Indiana Coronavirus Hotline** \- Get help with any questions you have regarding COVID-19 via Indiana's toll-free, 24/7 hotline! **Call 877-826-0011**!
* **FAQ's on Indiana's Stay-at-Home Order** \-- This FAQ has been updated to reflect questions on this week’s latest order. [Click here for answers to your questions](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4ac955cf87&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
* **File for Unemployment** \- Many Hoosiers are temporarily unemployed due to COVID-19. [Click here for information and to file for unemployment](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=d3904f0a15&e=1acd9bb3d6)!

We're in this together! Help one another, and share these resources with friends and family who need them!
---
|
---
|
---
| _**Election Update: June 2 Primary**_
Indiana's primary election date has been moved to Tuesday, June 2. During this primary, any registered voter has the option to cast their ballot via a mail-in absentee ballot.
[\--> Learn more about requesting an absentee ballot here <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=dd72781243&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Upcoming primary election dates for your calendar –**
**Monday, May 4: **Voter Registration Deadline **Tuesday, May 5: **Early Voting Begins In Person **Friday, May 8:** End of Pre-Primary Election Campaign Finance Reporting Period **Thursday, May 14:** Traveling Boards Begin **Thursday, May 21:** Deadline for a Request for an Absentee By-Mail Ballot to Be Received by the Clerk or Board of Elections and Registration **Sunday, May 31: **End of Pre-Primary Supplemental Campaign Finance Large Contribution Reporting Period **Monday, June 1: **Deadline for Traveling Boards & Noon Deadline for Early Voting **Tuesday, June 2:** Primary Day (noon is the deadline to return absentee ballots)
---
|
---
| | **_News You Can Use_** **Governor Eric Holcomb **[announces businesses that can stay open under executive order](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=10ec819eb5&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch **[discusses #INthistogether campaign](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c63a4abbaf&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Todd Young **[is calling on WHO chief to testify before Senate committee](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=885c050c4e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Senator Mike Braun**[talks business during pandemic](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f444aba06e&e=1acd9bb3d6)** ** ** **
**Congresswoman Jackie Walorski **[discusses Paycheck Protection Program](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=319d27f91b&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Jim Banks **[wants to bring criminal case to China over coronavirus coverup](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=34f2460abb&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congresswoman Susan Brooks**[discusses state, federal response to COVID-19](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=aab77bc70c&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Greg Pence **[says in the face of a challenge, Americans always unite as one](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=b7d925554a&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Larry Bucshon **[discusses Good Samaritan Health Professionals Act](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=00bb5a81be&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Congressman Trey Hollingsworth **[offers updated coronavirus resources](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=92bd4f35b6&e=1acd9bb3d6)

Infrastructure Updates: Vehicle traffic is down 40% during the week and over 50% on the weekends! Community Crossings grants were awarded this week, which assist local communities with the repair, ongoing maintenance, and improvement of their roads and bridges. 217 Community Crossing grants are being awarded in 61 counties and 156 cities and towns...for a total of $126.5 million in matching grants! to see if your county or community has been awarded funds! And with fewer Hoosiers on the roads, INDOT is jumpstarting road construction across the state. This includes: I-70 on the east side of Indianapolis will be closed for major repairs. Originally scheduled to take five months to complete, it will now be done in one month. Section Six of I-69 (between Martinsville and Indianapolis) continues to move forward as scheduled. Projects are occurring all over the state, including the Lloyd Expressway in Evansville and I-469 in Fort Wayne. To stay up to date on the latest developments, please make you're followingGovernor Holcomb on , and ! Thank you to every Hoosier who is doing his or her part to stop the spread of the coronavirus and flatten the curve. We're #INThisTogether! | | | Resources: A State That Works Together As news and information on the coronavirus continue to rapidly change -- we want to make sure you have the resources you need to stay up to date! Indiana State Department of Health \- The Indiana State Department of Health is posting important information, and frequent updates to their website. ! Indiana Coronavirus Hotline \- Get help with any questions you have regarding COVID-19 via Indiana's toll-free, 24/7 hotline! Call 877-826-0011! FAQ's on Indiana's Stay-at-Home Order \-- This FAQ has been updated to reflect questions on this week’s latest order. ! File for Unemployment \- Many Hoosiers are temporarily unemployed due to COVID-19. ! We're in this together! Help one another, and share these resources with friends and family who need them! | | | Election Update: June 2 Primary Indiana's primary election date has been moved to Tuesday, June 2. During this primary, any registered voter has the option to cast their ballot via a mail-in absentee ballot. Upcoming primary election dates for your calendar – Monday, May 4: Voter Registration Deadline Tuesday, May 5: Early Voting Begins In Person Friday, May 8: End of Pre-Primary Election Campaign Finance Reporting Period Thursday, May 14: Traveling Boards Begin Thursday, May 21: Deadline for a Request for an Absentee By-Mail Ballot to Be Received by the Clerk or Board of Elections and Registration Sunday, May 31: End of Pre-Primary Supplemental Campaign Finance Large Contribution Reporting Period Monday, June 1: Deadline for Traveling Boards & Noon Deadline for Early Voting Tuesday, June 2: Primary Day (noon is the deadline to return absentee ballots) | | | News You Can Use Governor Eric Holcomb Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch Senator Todd Young Senator Mike Braun Congresswoman Jackie Walorski Congressman Jim Banks Congresswoman Susan Brooks Congressman Greg Pence Congressman Larry Bucshon Congressman Trey Hollingsworth | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2020 Indiana Republican State Committee, Inc., All rights reserved.

The Georgia Secretary of State’s office has mailed out absentee ballot request forms to all active voters. You will need to mark the Democratic Party ballot and mail the form back to vote in this important U.S. Senate race.

Once officials receive your form, they will mail you the actual ballot.** And if you voted early in the Presidential Preference election, you must request this ballot to be able to vote in the Senate primary.**

Ensuring that as many voters as possible request their absentee ballot now is how we will protect the right to vote without needlessly risking public health by forcing in-person voting during a pandemic.

**[Today I’m asking you to commit to voting by mail in Georgia and wherever it is allowed across the country to stop the spread of this pandemic. Please join me in making this important commitment here:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17104556/182476226/-1424662828?contactdata=ISqkpjtEHfjxq73SDsrDcF2eGEsZqgRFYu4Xf0oMwzetI8tm8KHwZX%2B11PKTGX%2BFets8kS9xEVh9NSNr6V5Tj7G%2BVylGqMoqgXmPOVBZod3sry6%2B9NyvhEjKqbyMuiK67cjnDx3%2FyIFuyOEmHRq36qmqQu5QdWWDThpdXQmBLxQ%3D&quick=true&ms=em041020-e&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTM0NjM5Ni01ZjdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e3Xygjc4qe_CQEyrxJKsTKjKbskz46Z31F7bn0NaPMI=&emci=89346396-5f7b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a346396-5f7b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=463701)** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17104557/182476227/-1424662828?contactdata=ISqkpjtEHfjxq73SDsrDcF2eGEsZqgRFYu4Xf0oMwzetI8tm8KHwZX%2B11PKTGX%2BFets8kS9xEVh9NSNr6V5Tj7G%2BVylGqMoqgXmPOVBZod3sry6%2B9NyvhEjKqbyMuiK67cjnDx3%2FyIFuyOEmHRq36qmqQu5QdWWDThpdXQmBLxQ%3D&quick=true&ms=em041020-e&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NDI2NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTM0NjM5Ni01ZjdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=e3Xygjc4qe_CQEyrxJKsTKjKbskz46Z31F7bn0NaPMI=&emci=89346396-5f7b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=8a346396-5f7b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=463701) Social distancing during the COVID-19 pandemic is the most important action any one of us can take to flatten the curve and stop the spread of the coronavirus. I will not accept a scenario like the one Wisconsin’s Republican legislature cruelly and recklessly imposed on its voters with **in-person election conducted in only 5 polling places in the entire state.**

Governor Kemp closed polling places as Secretary of State presiding over his own election against Stacey Abrams — we must vote by mail in the highest proportion possible to protect our right to vote while remaining safe. Please join me.

Yours truly,
Teresa ****

June 23rd might seem far away, but in the blink of an eye, it will be at our doorstep, and we will be put to the test in the primary. **Here’s how you can help********** ** and be a part of this historic movement to defeat Mitch McConnell. **

**How we spend this time will truly determine the future of our campaign. **
If we want to move forward our fight for a Green New Deal, a Universal Basic Income, and economic justice to break the cycles of poverty — we need the resources to fund our grassroots strategy.
Our team has been working together to map out a plan to win and there’s a lot we need to get done before April ends.
With new digital advocacy and outreach initiatives, we’ve been able to grow our progressive movement. Building up these programs is critical if we want a fighting chance to go on and defeat Mitch in November.

name: Team Stanton
subject: Did you see Greg's message about stepping up to support the most vulnerable in our community?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
We just wanted to follow up to make sure you saw Greg's email from yesterday about supporting two non-profit organizations that are doing invaluable work to help Arizonans persevere through this difficult time, Meals on Wheels and The Restaurant Workers' Community Foundation.

**As Greg mentioned in his email that we forwarded below, these two organizations are helping some of the Arizonans most impacted by the COVID-19 crisis and he wants those with the means to do so, to step up and show their support during this crisis.**

[**Click here to join Greg in splitting a tax-deductible charitable contribution between Meals on Wheels and The Restaurant Workers ' Community Foundation >>**](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=25&recurring=auto)

_Charitable contributions made using this form will be split evenly between Meals on Wheels and The Restaurant Workers ' Community Foundation._

[__](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/ZSLJYNGpXBvItav_z2sFuSLubmorIAIEe3L0IQEs24U=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=25&recurring=auto)

Thank you for stepping up to support vulnerable Arizonans as we get through this crisis together!

_\-- Team Stanton_

* * *

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Greg Stanton** <info@stantonforarizona.com>
Date: Wed, Apr 8, 2020 at 5:00 PM
Subject: During this difficult time, will you join me in supporting some important non-profits?
To: <info@stantonforarizona.com>

As our community is currently fighting to persevere through this unprecedented COVID-19 public health crisis and economic fallout, I am calling for folks in our community with the resources to do so, to step up and join me in supporting our most vulnerable during this time.

**Among other organizations I am contributing to right now, I am making donations to support Meals on Wheels and the Restaurant Workers ' Community Foundation.**

Meals on Wheels does an invaluable service to our community in providing meals to those who do not have the financial means to afford them for their families, which is invaluable during this time of public health concern and economic difficulty for many here in Arizona.

The Restaurant Workers' Community Foundation has stepped up to support local small businesses, their employees, and those in their communities during this time of social distancing, which has really hurt the business of local restaurants and bars here, which are the backbones of our local communities.

[**Will you chip in here to join me in splitting a tax-deductible charitable contribution between Meals on Wheels and The Restaurant Workers ' Community Foundation?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=25&recurring=auto)

_Charitable contributions made using this form will be split evenly between Meals on Wheels and The Restaurant Workers ' Community Foundation._

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=5&recurring=auto)

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=25&recurring=auto)

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=50&recurring=auto)

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=100&recurring=auto)

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&amount=250&recurring=auto)

[****](https://act.myngp.com/el/uafnivjvc-EmQhjTAQorcImRgeaeURToy_j9T5QgbTo=/583dC192osCdRc2HjpCznn5YtSK2yadzRUyT8-yRjFs=?refcode=X_040920_NonProfits_B&recurring=auto)

There are so many other organizations that need our help during this time and I encourage you to show your support for them too.

Together, we will get through this difficult time as a strong and unified Arizona community.

\-- _Greg Stanton_

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Democrats Opt for Blackmail Over Small-Business Money—and the Media Covers for Them
body:
“What a deal for Democrats: They get to hold the economy for ransom, and the media blithely cover for them.” | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c1de0e0479&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Democrats Opt for Blackmail Over Small-Business Money—and the Media Covers for Them**

“What a deal for Democrats: They get to hold the economy for ransom, and the media blithely cover for them,” the _New York Post_ editorial board writes.

“On Thursday, Senate Democrats nixed a simple but urgent request to boost cash in the emergency small business loan program Congress set up this month from $350 billion to $600 billion.”

“Everything is an opportunity,” House Speaker Nancy Pelosi said, previewing Democrats’ plan to use the small business money as leverage for other items on their agenda.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=878db934ce&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Frequently lost in the current debate surrounding hydroxychloroquine and chloroquine — should these drugs be used as treatments for the coronavirus? — is the fundamental principle of giving hope to individuals with life-threatening diseases. The emergency use authorization granted by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for these two drugs provides one such avenue to give patients hope, as does key legislation signed into law two years ago by President Donald Trump,” Mary Vought writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6ac2875b30&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “This is fast becoming my favorite genre of journalism: A major news outlet will at first report accurately on a breaking news event, but then amend its own headline later in such a way as to make sure it does not a) reflect well on Republicans or b) reflect poorly on Democrats,” Becket Adams writes in the [_Washington Examiner_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6ec949b458&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|
---
| “We shouldn’t replicate what happened after the H1N1 health crisis by depleting our stockpile and failing to replenish items that were removed and used in the treatment of patients. President Trump, Vice President Mike Pence and their team will not repeat the mistake of the previous administration,” Rep. Andy Biggs (R-AZ) writes for [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d41eadf418&e=3ee4985eea).
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=43f4bdf2ab&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f3e43775c8&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=b43d02ef9f)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: C. Chase
subject: 53 Days Out – Campaign Update for Peter
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CLAIRE CHASE FOR CONGRESS INC
body:
---
| | | | | | [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001izrsdiPV38xjCB3qoev5Ez7EyUJ75c__bbysplxI1NzmkZQc0bCT0VZ84Ref40GLpYiIwky-Lunp5Hk0ktLTteow5vAs-LPNx2aJxyNXRLhIFokKPngROz8VdaJLICiW_G6mWN5rtM-8G7YERT_qPg==&c=QfSKiOAdKqUHB_ac6cWVcrS8-IQ9Ac-mctTJdErdpg469hN6_4JcnA==&ch=GvkrhUaar_NFzZM9UAWZ4qw04q9deT0mAG--AaWziqrtoX9FCsdH6g==)
---
| Hi Peter,
I hope you are having a blessed Good Friday. We're 53 days out from the primary election and I wanted to provide my biggest supporters with a quick update on the campaign and how we've been adjusting during the coronavirus pandemic.
We've Cancelled All In-Person Political Events
My favorite part of running for office has been traveling throughout the district, meeting incredible New Mexicans, and hearing YOUR IDEAS for how Washington could work better for New Mexico. The coronavirus forced us to make the painful, but necessary decision to indefinitely postpone all of our events around the district.
Instead, my team and I have been calling voters to let them know my priorities and answer any questions they may have about our campaign. Don't be surprised if you hear from me!
If you'd like to help us make calls, please contact Cam@ClaireChase.org.
We've Been Volunteering
Actions speak louder than words and people need help. Thankfully I'm in good health, so instead of campaigning we've been volunteering. This includes everything from giving blood to delivering Meals on Wheels to those who really need it.
Crises often bring out the best in Americans, and I'm thankful for everyone who is doing their part. If you know someone who needs a volunteer, please send me a note at claire@clairechase.org - I'd love to help.

We've Stopped All Fundraisers
Let's be honest: the worst part of running for office is asking people for money. Unfortunately, without campaign contributions it is impossible to communicate with voters through TV, radio, and mail. My team and I made the difficult decision to suspend all in-person fundraising, but that doesn't make the issues we are fighting for any less important.
Right now we are relying on our biggest online supporters for help. Let me be clear: If you are facing financial hardship, please do not give. [But if you have the means and want to help us fight for secure borders, the Second Amendment, and our conservative values, please consider contributing anything you can. Every dollar helps and all contributions are being 2X MATCHED until 11:59 PM tonight! ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001izrsdiPV38xjCB3qoev5Ez7EyUJ75c__bbysplxI1NzmkZQc0bCT0dyz-IC1k0uiLVvk9rODlttLwwC_zcN9D7OMTsTcUG6x2Y8uC0AWMORYZWebxowrKWOKOwvuu_50avhdMdUTV-Iu1pUfuZEFUB-5xa7waCT0r62zz9WbtapoHThhwyk6ig==&c=QfSKiOAdKqUHB_ac6cWVcrS8-IQ9Ac-mctTJdErdpg469hN6_4JcnA==&ch=GvkrhUaar_NFzZM9UAWZ4qw04q9deT0mAG--AaWziqrtoX9FCsdH6g==)
---
| Thanks for always coming through when it really matters.
---
|
---
| |
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001izrsdiPV38xjCB3qoev5Ez7EyUJ75c__bbysplxI1NzmkZQc0bCT0cTl9SPMeu6u_XyrBBkuC-CPMZNRttxjkSWiIoEmzW90D0XQZ8GZSV88C_3XzXh5US8iaITBKQ-JCWyGo1-EcQa8B-Rf07jnTLCbBPy39aEO&c=QfSKiOAdKqUHB_ac6cWVcrS8-IQ9Ac-mctTJdErdpg469hN6_4JcnA==&ch=GvkrhUaar_NFzZM9UAWZ4qw04q9deT0mAG--AaWziqrtoX9FCsdH6g==) [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001izrsdiPV38xjCB3qoev5Ez7EyUJ75c__bbysplxI1NzmkZQc0bCT0cTl9SPMeu6uTHfLxF1gyzfgmV34tJN9gvXpJVMYPEnc8uIuQeYs56w2fwR4GO2uxjZI43_IYNIXC0EZ5cePA-9cm6ZmO4bNluK6rKLkLMaW&c=QfSKiOAdKqUHB_ac6cWVcrS8-IQ9Ac-mctTJdErdpg469hN6_4JcnA==&ch=GvkrhUaar_NFzZM9UAWZ4qw04q9deT0mAG--AaWziqrtoX9FCsdH6g==) [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001izrsdiPV38xjCB3qoev5Ez7EyUJ75c__bbysplxI1NzmkZQc0bCT0cTl9SPMeu6uEwrYzbGXE38IzfsrAO3ukUHx_Ymt_8wWV0aFa5F8WlNGzkGvgFDpocFT8fDPed6_kyyxCpY0IRib--qsg3G1dsEGHzcYi_2hQgiZ9ehqSVs=&c=QfSKiOAdKqUHB_ac6cWVcrS8-IQ9Ac-mctTJdErdpg469hN6_4JcnA==&ch=GvkrhUaar_NFzZM9UAWZ4qw04q9deT0mAG--AaWziqrtoX9FCsdH6g==)

name: Indiana Democratic Party
subject: Fast Facts Friday
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party
body:
| | | |
---|---
| | **-April 10, 2020-** **-We launched a Vote by Mail Fund-** [****](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105183/182490854/1745149176?refcode=FFF4.10&amount=25&recurring=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=) For the first time, EVERY SINGLE HOOSIER voter can vote by mail in the June 2 primary election. That’s huge because Hoosiers shouldn’t have to put their health at risk to exercise their right to vote. Republicans still haven’t agreed to mail every voter an application to receive their ballot at home. But we're not waiting. We're raising money to expand our voter outreach. [If you are able to support expanding vote by mail to every Hoosier, can you chip in $10?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105184/182490855/-933379051?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=FFF4.10express_lane_10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=) **Need a hand applying to vote by mail?** Check out the vote-by-mail flowchart [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105185/182490856/1477133090?type=3&theater&emci=b61971b7-b975-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=) for deadlines and instructions on registering to vote, applying to vote by mail, and mailing in your ballot. You can also find a 30-second video on how to fill out your vote by mail application [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105186/182490857/-803653937?v=MnqlsMN5nVM&emci=b61971b7-b975-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=). **-Indiana continues to lag behind other states in testing-** **** Yesterday, COVID-19 [testing fell](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105187/182490858/1812867039?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) for the third day in a row. [Every single neighboring state](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105188/182490859/-1480067278?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) is testing more per 100,000 than Indiana. And on a per-capita basis, Indiana is the [39th state](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105189/182490860/1973755151?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) for testing. As the number of cases [continues to rise](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105190/182490861/204002683?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535), testing is a critical step in stopping the spread of COVID-19. But Gov. Holcomb is in no rush to guarantee COVID-19 tests for every Hoosier that needs them. This week, Holcomb claimed to be ["pouring it on"](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105191/182490862/686618896?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) in regard to Indiana's COVID-19 response. If he's "pouring it on" then why are we lagging behind all of our neighboring states and most of the country? **-Re p. GiaQuinta fights for paid sick leave-** **** House Democratic Leader Phil GiaQuinta has once again [urged Gov. Holcomb](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105192/182490863/700361926?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) to ensure that all employers are given 80 hours of paid sick leave. Guaranteed paid sick leave means that sick Hoosiers don’t have to choose between potentially exposing others to COVID-19 and their livelihood. Holcomb has asked that Hoosiers stay at home, but has done little to flatten the curve regarding the workplace. He has not only failed to ensure paid sick leave but also [dragged his feet](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105193/182490864/199468051?emci=dca2a282-1e70-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=) on enforcing that only essential businesses stay open. Without these workplace protections, workers and their families will remain vulnerable to COVID-19. **-As cases rise, PPE is not guaranteed-** **** Personal protective equipment is necessary to keep our first responders and health care providers safe. But as COVID-19 cases continue to rise in Indiana, the state's [ability to replenish PPE is uncertain](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105194/182490865/-209448177?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535). Gov. Holcomb has assured Hoosiers that he is [confident in Trump’s response](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105195/182490866/2036325753?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) to the pandemic. Still, health officials aren’t counting on Trump to deliver more PPE from the national stockpile. Gov. Holcomb, are you still confident in Trump’s response? **-COVID-19 Relief-** We have created a running list of ways you can help others as well as receive relief during this time; you can see what's going on statewide and in your county [here](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105196/182490867/125652814?emci=c9b5e56e-ff74-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=ea000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001&ceid=4925535&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=). **-Save the date-** Allen County: [Meet the candidates during the Allen County Candidate Breakfast Meeting](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105198/182490869/1647350137?active_tab=about&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) \- April 11, 10 AM EST, Virtual Wells County: [Democratic Party April Meeting](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/17105200/182490871/1444075142?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4NTk5NjRlYi02NTdiLWVhMTEtYTk0Yy0wMDE1NWQwM2IxZTgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=X53JU5UChfr42mX-6nB7k9AVlOdI9i7ETTzSijGCBMw=&emci=f7bebd6e-317b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&emdi=859964eb-657b-ea11-a94c-00155d03b1e8&ceid=4925535) - body:
What happened in Wisconsin this week was a disgrace -- with hundreds of polling places shut-down, hours-long lines to vote, and people not getting their absentee ballots in time to vote safely from... | | |
---
What happened in Wisconsin this week was a disgrace -- with hundreds of polling places shut-down, hours-long lines to vote, and people not getting their absentee ballots in time to vote safely from home.

Let's be clear: Right-wing politicians are taking advantage of the coronavirus crisis to kick their voter suppression efforts into high gear.

But we're fighting back.

**Earlier this week, Elizabeth laid out how we'll be organizing and calling on government officials to demand safe voting practices -- but according to our records, you haven't signed up to join this new fight yet.

[Add your name and fight by Elizabeth's side to make our elections safe and secure.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257a0/45c30650/1f4732e28/66e9664e/4118630909/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwOFBfU0dOX0tJQ19UXzIwMjAwNDEwX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-wd-protect-elections?source=EMQ08P_SGN_KIC_T_20200410_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

And keep reading for more details from Elizabeth.

Thanks,

Warren Democrats

---

\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Elizabeth Warren** <info@elizabethwarren.com>
Date: Tue, Apr 7, 2020
Subject: Add your name: Make sure every American can vote safely in a pandemic
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
---
Voting is the foundation of our democracy.

And no one should have to choose between protecting their health and making their voice heard.

But with the coronavirus crisis making it dangerous to gather in public places, wait in long lines, and touch shared surfaces like voting machines, we need a plan to make sure Americans can vote safely in a pandemic.

That's why today, I'm outlining simple steps that Congress needs to take in its next coronavirus relief package to protect the health and safety of America's voters. Here are a few of them:
* Universal vote by mail in every state
* Online and same-day voter registration so that everyone can make their voice heard
* At least 30 days of early voting and extended voting hours to enhance the safety of in-person voting
* Guarantee that every poll worker is compensated -- and receives hazard pay -- for their work
* Provide at least $4 billion for state and local governments to administer elections and to keep poll workers safe

There's more, but it all comes down to this: **During a pandemic, we need voters to stay safe, but not be silenced. And our leaders -- from Congress to governors and state officials -- need to act _now_ to lay the groundwork for safe and secure elections in November.**

**[Over the coming weeks, we'll be calling on governors, local officials, and the federal government to make sure every American can safely exercise their right to vote. Add your name to join the fight and get more updates on how you can take action.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257a0/45c30650/1f4732e28/66e9664e/4118630909/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwOFBfU0dOX0tJQ19UXzIwMjAwNDEwX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-wd-protect-elections?source=EMQ08P_SGN_KIC_T_20200410_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

We've already seen Republicans use the crisis to accelerate an undemocratic power grab and disenfranchise millions.

Last week, Donald Trump claimed that if we made it easier for more people to vote, "you'd never have a Republican elected in this country again."

And he's just the latest in a string of right-wing politicians who've embraced efforts to make it harder for people to vote -- by closing polling places, purging millions of voters from voting rolls, limiting early voting, and passing restrictive voter identification laws.

But in times of crisis, when people's lives and livelihoods are on the line, it's more important than ever that elected representatives are held accountable. There's no room for partisanship when it comes to protecting the basic machinery of our democracy.

We need to make sure every voter can safely make their voice heard, including by taking these steps:
* Expand online voter registration so it's an option in every state, extend voter registration deadlines as much as practicable, and let any eligible voter register on Election Day.

* Make states provide at least 30 days of early voting to help reduce crowds and maintain access to safe, in-person voting.

* Mail every registered voter a ballot and a postage-paid return envelope. That way, they can safely fill it out at home, send it back in, and participate in our democracy.

* Ban states from purging voter rolls during this pandemic -- unless a voter has come forward and said they want to be removed, or unless there's objective evidence that their registration is out of date. And states must add voters' names back to the rolls if they've already been purged.

* Ensure equal access to the polls -- regardless of race or ethnicity, language, tribal citizenship, or ability. That includes ensuring sufficient and safe polling places on Election Day, providing accessible ballot machines for people with disabilities, expanding voting hours, and allowing people to vote with a sworn statement of identity instead of an ID.

* Provide no less than $4 billion in surge funding to states for election administration, compensate every poll worker (including providing hazard pay), and bolster election security.

By taking these steps -- and more -- we'll ensure elections can proceed safely, securely, and on time.

**Congress needs to act, but governors and state officials must do their part, too -- and as a movement, we'll be calling on them to do just that.

I'll keep up the fight in Washington, but I can't do it alone -- we can only protect our democracy if we fight side by side.

[Sign up now to say you're in this fight.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285257a0/45c30650/1f4732e28/66e9664e/4118630909/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVEwOFBfU0dOX0tJQ19UXzIwMjAwNDEwX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-wd-protect-elections?source=EMQ08P_SGN_KIC_T_20200410_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")**

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

name: Admiral Mike
subject: Major Endorsement:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Franken for Iowa
body:
We have great news, crew! Congresswoman -- and former US Navy Commander -- Elaine Luria (VA-02) endorsed me in my bid for the US Senate. I am deeply grateful and honored to earn her support.
|
---
| |
---

| | We have great news, crew! Congresswoman -- and former US Navy Commander -- Elaine Luria (VA-02) endorsed me in my bid for the US Senate. I am deeply grateful and honored to earn her support. [](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f249e/33365068/4a765c54/3f8bf3fb/2023143381/VEsH/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mf2020q2?refcode=FRA_20200409_luria_em_fr_all_img") Congresswoman Luria is a leader. She spent 20 years as a Navy Surface Warfare Officer -- becoming the executive officer of a guided missile cruiser and the commander of Assault Craft Unit Two -- leading hundreds of sailors at the end of her Naval career. Now, she represents Coastal Virginia in Congress, a district that encompasses the largest naval base complex and veteran constituencies in the country. **She has made it her mission to represent veterans, promote national security, and protect service members worldwide.** She's made climate resiliency -- developing our infrastructure to withstand floods, high winds, and storms -- a top priority to protect the people of her district. She's set an example for a leader Americans everywhere deserve, and I'll bring that same energy and dedication as the next Senator for Iowa. And as the next US Senator from Iowa, I'll make it a priority to work closely with Congresswoman Luria for our veterans, and help protect our citizens and the economy from climate change. But I'll need your support, as well as Congresswoman Luria's, to get there. [**Click here, and please make a donation to support my campaign.**](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f249e/33365068/4a765c54/3f8bf3f4/2023143381/VEsE/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mf2020q2?refcode=FRA_20200409_luria_em_fr_all_l1") _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5 >>](https://act.frankenforiowa.org/page/m/b7f249e/33365068/4a765c54/3f8bf3f5/2023143381/VEsF/?g=DOn8TVNw52hdfH9rXQFzC_A "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mf2020q2?refcode=FRA_20200409_luria_em_fr_all_abx5&express_lane=true")

name: =?utf-8?Q?Catherine=20Engelbrecht?=
subject: UPDATE: The Fight to Protect Voters' Rights
body:
This week New Mexico voters pushed back against rights violations | | | Hello Everyone and **Happy Easter!**

See below for quick update on what happened this week in the New Mexico case.

To all of the concerned citizens who've contacted us from other states where lawsuits have been filed to restrict voters' rights - thank you, we are with you!

To all of you who have generously supported us - **thank you!** Every dollar is being immediately deployed to efforts on the ground and in the courts!

To all of you who've contacted us to volunteer, thank you! We are working to put together local election integrity teams or connect you with existing state groups doing great work. Please be patient with us as all this comes together! More news to follow on many fronts.....

Onward -
Catherine

---
| [ ](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=7a09a13615&e=ad8cba7e98)
---
| | **UPDATE: True the Vote Fights Against Court-Controlled Mail-in Elections**
On November 8th, an amicus brief was filed on behalf of concerned New Mexico voters in a lawsuit where 27 county clerks and the Secretary of State, without opposition, request the Supreme Court of New Mexico to order that the June 2 primary election occur by mail-in ballot only and eliminate in-person and absentee ballot voting. This strategic move by democrats would remove all voter fraud protections provided in in-person and absentee voting. The government officials based their entire request on the national health crisis caused by the Covid-19 virus.

The New Mexico voters file their brief to assert their fundamental right to vote under the State and Federal Constitution---including the right to not have one’s vote diluted by votes of illegal voters. The voters argue that the Supreme Court should decline the request of undoing all current election laws and instead protect the integrity of the state’s primary elections from voter fraud and preserve the rights of every eligible and registered voter.
[](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=5161d90543&e=ad8cba7e98)
An election conducted via mail-in ballot, as proposed in New Mexico, would strip away substantial protections against voter fraud contained in procedures for in-person voting and absentee ballots and would all but ensure an election fraught with both ballot fraud and vote harvesting. Under this scheme, the legal safeguards that are attendant to in-person and absentee voting would be dispensed with entirely. These politicians assert that there is no alternative to their proposal that would not endanger the public health or prevent New Mexicans from exercising their right to vote.

Catherine Engelbrecht, President of True the Vote, the organization funding the voters efforts in fighting the lawsuit, remarks: “These government officials are spearheading a nationally concerted effort by a radicalized Deep State to dispense with any notion of fairness in elections. True the Vote and our allies are on the front lines to fight for voters and preserve our state election laws.”

James Bopp, Jr., of The Bopp Law Firm PC, lead counsel for the New Mexican Voters, and General Counsel for True the Vote, says: “There are national implications for voters nationwide. There is no precedent for such an extraordinary relief. Should these Democrat politicians succeed here, it would create a precedent for other state courts to follow in New Mexico’s footsteps and to impose similarly unprotected and unrestricted voting procedures though the nearly unlimited equitable power of the courts.”
A full copy of the amicus brief may be found [here](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=74e1513f3f&e=ad8cba7e98)
---
| | | | | | [](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=a5ae9118da&e=ad8cba7e98)
---
| | [](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/track/click?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=27043c88df&e=ad8cba7e98)
---

---

You can [update your preferences](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/profile?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=65fa0bffcb&e=ad8cba7e98) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://truethevote.us8.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=d9ab56570d4ecf7b7df9d4c25&id=65fa0bffcb&e=ad8cba7e98&c=ee21a43c2e).

name: Governor Eric Holcomb
subject: Happy Easter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana
body:
Sacrifice, faith, and family I reflect on these values every single Easter, and
this one will be no different. In fact, it’s even more important this

<>
Friend,

Sacrifice, faith, and family.

I reflect on these values every single Easter, and this one will be no
different — in fact, it’s even more important this year. Because of the risk
posed by COVID-19, Hoosiers won’t be able to flock to the pews of our churches,
and we won’t all be able to gather our families together to say a prayer over
an Easter meal.

This is a new challenge for us all — especially during this Holy Week. But the
church is a body, not a building — and we’ll come together with church
livestreams to celebrate the Word and the Truth.

In times of uncertainty, it’s even more vital to turn to our faith for
guidance. I have been taking solace in the words of Matthew 18:20.

"For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.”

I can’t think of a verse more appropriate for this Easter. Even though we
cannot join together with all our family and friends, we can still celebrate
our faith and reflect on our core values.

Last Sunday as we celebrated Palm Sunday, the palm branches thrown at Jesus’
feet as he was entering Jerusalem symbolized peace and victory. We, too, will
see victory, and we will see peace when we complete this difficult journey.

And trust me, we’ll emerge on the other side of this crisis with a state that
is stronger than ever before.

Happy Easter - stay safe, stay healthy, and let’s stay #INThisTogether.

-Eric

Paid for by Eric Holcomb for Indiana. © Copyright 2020. All rights reserved.

Eric Holcomb for Indiana, 101 W Ohio Street, Suite 2200, Indianapolis, IN
46204 | (317) 569-0709

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Kathy Manning
subject: This crisis requires immediate action:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kathy Manning for Congress
body:
|
---

| | This is unacceptable -- we're in the midst of a global health pandemic, but the Trump Administration refuses to reopen Affordable Care Act enrollment so uninsured Americans can sign up for coverage and get the care they need. More than 30 million Americans are uninsured, and more than 6.6 million Americans have filed for unemployment in the wake of the coronavirus outbreak. For many, that means they are now without coverage, too. **This is a crisis, and it calls for immediate action. Reopening ACA enrollment is an important step we need to take to make sure millions of American can access care if they get sick.** [_**Will you add your name to mine and call on the Trump Administration and Republicans to stop their attacks on health care and reopen ACA enrollment for Americans in need? We need 5,000 signatures before midnight tonight.**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d36c/3d199a51/2f79ac61/7364f337/3095815428/VEsH/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200410p_z") Americans are afraid of not being able to afford their medical bills. And rightfully so -- the average cost for treatment for the coronavirus could add up to more than $20,000. I understand their fears. When my youngest daughter was diagnosed with a chronic illness, our insurance company refused to cover the medication she needed and said it would cost us $10,000 per month out of pocket. [_**No family should have to go through that -- we need to make sure everyone can access the health care they need. Will you be one of the 5,000 grassroots supporters to sign our petition before midnight?**_](https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/page/m/18d9d36c/3d199a51/2f79ac61/7364f337/3095815428/VEsE/?g=Bm8FRTjfd5ld5gouxTH21cA "https://secure.kathymanningfornc.com/e200410p_z") Thank you, Kathy |
---

---

---
